other_observers: Cole Wild, Sue Riffe, Dan Laszlo, Bill Miller, Jeff Stark, Gail Kratz, 2 others (name unknown);; Nick Komar and Cole Wild originally found and identified the bird simultaneously. Ruth Dick and one other saw the bird independently later that afternoon (and reported the sighting to COBIRDS).
specific_location: 6.8 miles south of Briggsdale on Colorado highway 392, about 0.9 mi south of hte intersection with County Road 80, on west side of road.
description: A Kingbird-sized flycatcher with an exceptionally long tail flew up to and perched on a fence wire. The bird was mostly whitish-gray below, with a salmon wash to the lower underparts, pale gray on the upperparts with a dark gray line through a black eye, small black bill, and a forked black-and-white tail about equal in length (or slightly greater) to the bird. Length of the body was estimated at 7 inches; thus total length was estimated at 14-16 inches.Behaviors: Perched on a wire Kingbird-style with frequent sallies presumably for catching insects. Alternated between perching on the fence wire and on low vegetation (weed-like vegetation) a few inches from the ground.Call: not callingPlumage: Adult
